w:r.w:t: Code Changes | Functionality | Flow Issues | What to Implement

Gixbee is an Intent-Based multi-service platform that unifies event planning, gig hiring, job discovery, and vendor listing under one application. Unlike role-based systems, Gixbee lets a single user book a hall, work as a driver, and list their own business — all in the same session.
After Login → OTP verification → Name registration, the user is presented with four intent-based entry points:
Find halls, catering, decoration, photography, rentals & instant help workers
Talent discovery — apply for jobs, attend interviews, track status
Live Worker Engine — offer skills, get real-time gig work, earn hourly
Register service, hiring, or rental businesses; manage units & operators
Gixbee is a facilitator — it never assigns workers or vendors. Every action (accept, confirm, approve) is a conscious choice by the user. This is the Safe Harbor structure across all modules.
This is the largest customer-facing module. It splits into two distinct flows after the user selects the event/service location:
Calendar-based booking for Hall, Catering, Decoration, Photography, Rental. Location-driven (event location, not user location).
Real-time gig worker dispatch for Electrician, Driver, Cleaning, Nurse. Uses the Live Worker Engine.
Complete step-by-step flow with code requirements:

User selects WHERE the event is (not their current location). System filters all services to that district/city. Code: store selectedEventLocation in session/state. Filter service queries by this location.
User picks Hall / Catering / Decoration / Photography / Rental. Code: route to corresponding service listing screen with location param.
Show vendor cards with photos, capacity, description. Code: GET /services?type={type}&location={location}. Display availability badge.
Tap 'Check Availability' opens calendar. Slots shown as: Available (green), Pending (yellow), Booked (red). Code: GET /calendar/{vendorId} — return blocked dates array.
User selects Package (fixed price) OR Custom (flexible requirements). Two separate request flows must be implemented.
State set to REQUESTED. Vendor gets push notification. Code: POST /bookings {type:'package', vendorId, date, packageId}. Status: REQUESTED.
User fills event type, guest count, special needs. Code: POST /bookings {type:'custom', vendorId, date, details:{...}}. Status: CUSTOM_REQUESTED.
Vendor Approves or Rejects. If custom: vendor sends quote + notes. Code: PATCH /bookings/{id}/status {action:'approve'|'reject', quote?, notes?}.
User reviews quote (if custom) and confirms. State: CONFIRMED. Code: PATCH /bookings/{id}/confirm. Calendar date is then blocked.
Once CONFIRMED, date blocked for that vendor unit. Code: Insert date into blocked_dates for vendorId. Reflect immediately on calendar.
User picks Current Location OR Another Location. If another: enter city/address. Code: store serviceLocation; if GPS use device coords, else geocode input.

System asks: 'Who will be present at the location?' User selects Self OR Someone Else. If someone else: collect Name, Relation, Phone — this becomes On-Site Contact. Code: store onSiteContact in booking record.
System finds available workers within radius for selected skill. Code: GET /workers?skill={skill}&lat={lat}&lng={lng}&radius=10km. Sort by distance & rating.
System sends job request to multiple workers simultaneously. Code: Push notification via FCM to matched worker devices. Timer: 90 seconds to accept.
First accepting worker gets the job. Code: PATCH /jobs/{id}/accept — set workerId, status=ACCEPTED. Reject others.
Customer sees worker profile and confirms. Code: PATCH /jobs/{id}/confirm — status=CONFIRMED. Movement monitoring starts.
7-min reminder sent to worker. At 10 min, system checks GPS movement. No movement detected: auto-cancel + strike added. Code: Background job / cron + GPS polling API.
Worker taps 'Arrived'. OTP sent to Customer or On-Site Contact. Worker enters OTP to start work. Code: Generate 4-digit OTP, store with 5-min TTL, verify before marking STARTED.
Worker taps 'Finish'. Completion OTP sent. Customer ticks 'Work Completed'. Code: Second OTP flow — verify, then set status=COMPLETED.
Customer pays. Minimum: 1 hour billing. Code: Calculate hours (ceil to 1hr minimum), show amount, process payment gateway.

After first job: worker receives Rs.12 welcome bonus. Must maintain Rs.12 wallet balance to go live again. Code: Check wallet_balance >= 12 before allowing Active toggle. Deduct on each new job accept.

The system currently does not clearly separate 'event location' from 'user current location'. Both Plan Services and Instant Help need distinct location capture flows. Additionally, the Presence Check / On-Site Contact screen is not yet implemented — this is critical for the Arrival OTP to be sent to the correct person.
Rental is a sub-category under Plan Services — NOT under Instant Help. It involves delivery logistics, so it requires calendar-based planning, not real-time dispatch.
User sets event/rental delivery location. Same location picker as Plan Services.
User sees categories: Generator, Mic Set, Cooler, Pressure Washer, etc. Code: GET /rentals?location={location}&category={category}.
Photos, specs, hourly rate, minimum hours shown. Code: GET /rentals/{itemId}. Display pricing: Rs.X/hour, min Y hours.
Calendar shows Available / Booked days. Code: GET /rentals/{itemId}/calendar — return blocked dates.
User taps Request Rental with optional note. Status: REQUESTED. Code: POST /rental-bookings {itemId, date, note?}.
Vendor approves or rejects. Code: PATCH /rental-bookings/{id}/status.
User confirms. Status: CONFIRMED. Entire day blocked in calendar. Code: PATCH /rental-bookings/{id}/confirm. Block full day in item calendar.
After confirmation, customer and vendor coordinate directly (no OTP). Payment: Offline / GPay. Code: No further system intervention — only calendar block needed.
Minimum hours apply. Use 3hrs on 4hr minimum → pay 4hrs. Use 6hrs → pay 6hrs. Implement as: max(actual_hours, min_hours) * hourly_rate.
Entire day blocked on confirmation (to avoid return-delay conflicts). No partial-day slots for rental items.
Rental does not use Arrival/Completion OTP. Coordination is direct between parties after confirmation.
The Talent Discovery Engine. Connects students, freshers, and experienced professionals directly with hiring companies — no agencies, no newspaper ads.
User fills Education, Skills, Experience. Code: POST /talent/profile {education[], skills[], experience[]}. Store in talent_profiles table.
User selects: Final Year / Graduate / Experienced. Code: Store status field. Used for employer filtering.
User adds preferred roles (Nurse, Telecaller, etc.) and preferred work locations. Code: Store preferred_roles[], preferred_locations[].
User enables alert toggle. Code: Set job_alerts_enabled = true. Trigger: when new job matches talent's skills + location, send push notification.
Talent taps Apply on a job post. Status: APPLIED. Code: POST /applications {jobId, talentId}. Status=APPLIED.
Employer views applicants list. Selects for Interview or Rejects. Code: PATCH /applications/{id} {status: 'INTERVIEW' | 'REJECTED'}.
States: APPLIED → INTERVIEW → SELECTED | REJECTED. Talent sees live updates. Code: WebSocket or polling for status changes. Push notification on each state change.

If talent accepts interview but doesn't attend: system adds negative mark. Repeated = lower search priority. Code: Track interview_accepted + attended. If accepted but !attended: increment no_show_count. Apply rank penalty in search query.
Current Flow Issue — Find a Job

The negative behaviour control (no-show tracking) is not yet linked to the search ranking algorithm. Currently, a talent who repeatedly skips interviews still appears at the top of employer search results. The ranking query must factor in no_show_count as a penalty weight.
The real-time gig earning system. Workers register skills, get verified, set rates, go live, and earn hourly — without any middlemen.
Worker adds skills: Electrician, Driver, Cleaner, Nurse, etc. Each skill requires individual verification. Code: POST /worker/skills {skill, documents[]}. Status: VERIFICATION_PENDING.
Admin reviews proof documents and approves. Code: Admin panel PATCH /admin/skills/{id}/approve. Webhook triggers skill activation.
Worker sets hourly rate within system range. Can update max 2 times per day. Code: Store rate_updated_count per day. Reset at midnight. Reject if count >= 2.
Worker sets weekly schedule: Mon 9am-5pm, Tue 3pm-7pm, etc. Code: Store availability_schedule as JSON. Use to filter workers during customer search.
Worker toggles Active. System checks: wallet_balance >= 12 (except first job). Code: GET /worker/wallet. If balance < 12 AND has_done_first_job: show 'Top up Rs.12 to go live'. Else allow.
Customer triggers search. Matching workers receive push notification. Code: FCM push with job details. Worker has 90 seconds to accept.
Worker taps Accept. First to accept gets the job. Code: Atomic DB transaction — set job.worker_id if null. Return conflict error to slower acceptors.
Worker arrives, taps Arrived. OTP sent to on-site contact. Worker enters OTP. Code: Generate 6-digit OTP, store with 5-min TTL. SMS/WhatsApp to contact number.
Worker taps Finish. Second OTP. Customer confirms. Payment calculated. Code: Billing = max(actual_hours, 1) * hourly_rate. Process payment.
After first job: credit Rs.12 to worker wallet. Code: Check is_first_job flag. Credit wallet, set is_first_job = false.
Worker gets a strike for: accepting job then not moving (auto-cancel), or repeated no-shows. Implement worker_strikes table. After 3 strikes: suspend account.
Hourly rate update: max 2 changes per day. Enforce via rate_update_log table with date + count. Reset count at midnight IST.
Minimum Rs.12 balance required to go live (after first job). Deduct Rs.12 on each job acceptance. Refund if job cancelled before arrival.
Allows service providers and companies to register and manage their offerings. Three business categories, each with independent unit management.
Unit-Based Model: One owner can manage multiple independent business units of the same type.
User selects: Service Business → Hall/Catering/Decoration/Photography. Code: Route to service type selector.
System checks: does this user already have units of this type? Code: GET /businesses?ownerId={uid}&type={type}. If none: show Add Unit form.
User fills Company Name, Location, Description, uploads proof. Status: VERIFICATION_PENDING. Code: POST /businesses {type, name, location, description, documents[]}.
Admin verifies and approves. Code: PATCH /admin/businesses/{id}/approve. Status: VERIFIED. Push notification to owner.
User sees Unit Cards — one card per business. Plus Add New button. Code: GET /businesses?ownerId={uid}&type={type}. Render card grid.
Clicking a card: Manage Calendar, Add Capacity, Add Offline Days, Edit Details, Delete, Transfer Ownership, Add Operators. Code: Each action = separate API endpoint on /businesses/{id}/*.
Owner adds Manager. Manager can approve bookings and manage availability. Cannot transfer ownership. Code: POST /businesses/{id}/operators {userId, role:'manager'}. Permission middleware checks role.
Owner initiates transfer. OTP required from both parties. 24-hour hold applied. Code: POST /businesses/{id}/transfer {newOwnerId}. OTP verification flow. Cron job lifts hold after 24hrs.
Company Name, Business Type, Location, GST/Legal proof. Status: VERIFICATION_PENDING. Code: POST /hiring-businesses {name, type, location, documents[]}.
Owner creates department units (e.g., Development Team, Support Team). Each hires independently. Code: POST /hiring-businesses/{id}/units {name, description}.
Owner adds HR managers as Operators. HR can post jobs, view applicants, move candidates. Cannot transfer ownership. Code: POST /hiring-businesses/{id}/operators {userId, role:'hr'}.
HR posts role with: title, experience, salary, location, skills required. Code: POST /jobs {businessId, unitId, title, experience, salary, location, skills[]}.
On job post: system queries talent_profiles where skills overlap AND preferred_locations match. Send push alert. Code: Background job after job creation. Batch push via FCM.
HR moves candidates: APPLIED → INTERVIEW → SELECTED / REJECTED. Code: PATCH /applications/{id} {status, note?}. Each state change notifies talent.
Same 24-hour OTP hold as Service Business. Code: Shared ownership transfer service.
Vendor lists equipment: Generator, Mic Set, Cooler, Pressure Washer, etc. Code: POST /rental-items {vendorId, name, category, specs, hourlyRate, minHours, photos[]}.
Each item has its own calendar. Vendor sets availability. Code: GET/POST /rental-items/{id}/calendar.
Customer sends rental request (with optional note). Vendor reviews. Code: GET /rental-bookings?vendorId={id}&status=REQUESTED.
Vendor approves → customer confirms → day blocked. Code: PATCH /rental-bookings/{id}/status {action: 'approve'|'reject'}. On confirm: block date in calendar.
The following issues have been identified across all modules based on the intended system design vs. current implementation state:
No separation between user's current location and event location. Both Plan Services and Instant Help show user's GPS as default for everything.
Implement two distinct location capture flows: selectedEventLocation for Plan Services; serviceLocation (GPS or manual) for Instant Help.
Presence Check screen not implemented. No way to add an on-site contact person. Arrival OTP always goes to booker — wrong if someone else is on-site.
Build Presence Check UI: 'I will be present' / 'Someone else'. If someone else: Name + Relation + Phone form. Store as on_site_contact in booking. Route OTP to this number.
No background GPS movement check implemented after worker accepts. Worker can accept and stay stationary with no consequence.
Implement background job: 7-min push reminder, 10-min GPS check. If no movement detected: auto-cancel job, add worker strike. Use scheduled task + GPS API.
Currently only single booking requests are supported. System does not allow a customer to send the same booking request to multiple vendors simultaneously.
Allow parallel booking requests to multiple vendors. Status: REQUESTED for each. First vendor to approve + customer confirms = CONFIRMED. Cancel others automatically.
No-show tracking exists but is not linked to talent search ranking. Repeated no-show talent still appears at top of employer results.
Add penalty weight to talent search query. Deduct search_rank score based on no_show_count. Weight formula: base_score - (no_show_count * penalty_factor).
Rs.12 activation requirement exists in spec but wallet balance check before going live is not implemented. Worker can go live with zero balance.
Before allowing Active toggle: if has_done_first_job == true, check wallet_balance >= 12. Block toggle and show top-up prompt if insufficient.
Workers can update their hourly rate unlimited times per day. No daily rate-change cap enforced.
Log each rate update in rate_update_log with timestamp. Count updates for today. Reject if count >= 2 with message: 'Rate can only be updated twice per day.'
List My Business — Ownership Transfer Hold
24-hour hold after ownership transfer is designed but the cron/scheduled job to lift the hold and finalise ownership is not implemented.
Implement scheduled job: 24hrs after transfer initiation, verify both OTPs were confirmed, then set new_owner as owner. If not confirmed within 48hrs, expire the request.
No permission middleware enforces what operators can/cannot do. An operator can currently access ownership transfer endpoints.
Add role-based permission middleware. Check user role per business unit before each action. Operators: bookings + calendar only. Owners: all actions including transfer.
Push notification triggers (job alert, booking status, application status) are not connected to FCM/APNs. Status changes update DB but no notification is sent.
Integrate Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM). Create notification service. Trigger pushes on: booking status change, job request received, application state change, OTP delivery.
Based on the architecture documents and flow analysis, the following features must be built:
POST /bookings — Package and Custom booking request (separate type field)
PATCH /bookings/{id}/status — Vendor approve/reject with quote support
GET /workers?skill&lat&lng&radius — Live worker search with availability filter
POST /otp/arrival and /otp/completion — OTP generation + SMS/WhatsApp delivery
GET /worker/wallet — Balance check before going live
POST /businesses/{id}/transfer — Ownership transfer with OTP + 24hr hold
PATCH /applications/{id} — Pipeline state machine for hiring
Location Picker — Dual mode: Event Location (Plan Services) vs. Service Location (Instant Help)
Presence Check Screen — Self / Someone Else + On-Site Contact form
Calendar View — 3-state visual: Available (green), Pending (yellow), Booked (red)
Booking Type Selector — Package card vs. Custom request card
Custom Booking Form — Event type, guest count, special needs
Quote Review Screen — Show vendor's quote + notes before confirm
Worker Live Map — Real-time worker movement tracking for customer
OTP Entry Screen — 4/6-digit input with resend option
Business Unit Dashboard — Card grid with Add New + per-unit management
Operator Management Screen — Add/remove operators with role display
Job Pipeline View (Hiring) — Kanban-style: Applied | Interview | Selected | Rejected
Talent Profile Builder — Education + Skills + Experience + Preferences
Wallet Top-Up Prompt — Rs.12 minimum balance screen before going live
Movement Monitor Job — 7-min reminder, 10-min GPS check, auto-cancel if no movement
Talent Job Alert Matcher — On new job post, match talent profiles and batch push
Ownership Transfer Finalizer — Cron: after 24hrs, finalize ownership transfer
Rate Update Reset — Midnight cron: reset rate_update_count per worker
FCM Integration — Push notification service connected to all status change events
Strike System — Track worker strikes; suspend at threshold
No-Show Penalty — Weight talent search ranking by no_show_count
id, type (package|custom), vendorId, customerId, date, status, quote, notes, on_site_contact (JSON)
id, itemId, vendorId, customerId, date, status, note, billing_hours
id, skill, serviceLocation, customerId, workerId, onSiteContact (JSON), status, arrivedAt, completedAt, billingHours
id, userId, skills[], availability_schedule (JSON), hourlyRate, is_active, wallet_balance, is_first_job, no_show_count, strike_count
id, workerId, updated_at, date (for daily count)
id, ownerId, type (service|hiring|rental), serviceType, name, location, status, operators (JSON: [{userId, role}])
id, businessId, fromOwnerId, toOwnerId, initiatedAt, otp_from, otp_to, status, finalizedAt
id, userId, education[], skills[], experience[], preferred_roles[], preferred_locations[], status, no_show_count, search_rank
id, jobId, talentId, status, interview_accepted, attended, applied_at
id, userId, type, payload (JSON), sent_at, read_at
Gixbee never assigns. Every booking, acceptance, and hiring decision is made by the user. System only facilitates matching and communication.
A user is not locked into a single role. The same account can book services, work as a gig worker, and list a business simultaneously.
All service discovery is location-first. Plan Services: event location. Instant Help: service location. Jobs: preferred work location.
Two OTP gates in Live Worker flow: Arrival OTP (work begins) and Completion OTP (work ends). Both sent to on-site contact, not necessarily the booker.
Confirmed bookings (Hall, Catering, Decoration, Photography, Rental) block the calendar immediately. Prevents double-booking.
One user can own multiple businesses of the same or different types. Each unit is independent — separate calendar, operators, and management.
Operators (Managers, HR) can manage day-to-day work but cannot transfer ownership. Ownership remains with the registered owner until OTP-verified transfer.
Skills (workers), businesses, and rental items all require admin approval before going live. Status flow: PENDING → VERIFIED / REJECTED.
Gixbee is a comprehensive, multi-dimensional platform. The core architecture is sound and well-designed. The following is the current implementation status:
Calendar view, multi-vendor parallel requests, quote flow
Presence check, movement monitor, both OTP gates
Day-block calendar, min-hour billing, no-OTP coordination
No-show ranking penalty, real-time status push
Rs.12 wallet gate, rate limit enforcement, strike system
Operator permissions middleware, ownership transfer cron
Job pipeline state machine, FCM job alerts

Prioritise: (1) FCM push notification infrastructure, (2) Presence Check + On-Site Contact, (3) Calendar view with correct state colours, (4) Rs.12 wallet gate for Live Worker. These four unblock the core user journeys across all modules.
